message:
feat: add --prefix flag for cross-rig issue creation

Adds --prefix as a forgiving alias to --rig flag. Accepts:
- Exact prefix: bd-, gt-
- Prefix without hyphen: bd, gt
- Rig name: beads, gastown

All resolve to the correct rig via routes.jsonl lookup.
This matches agents' mental model of prefix-based routing.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1dez.7","title":"Formula versioning: Version field and git tag integration","description":"Add versioning support to formulas for tracking updates.

## Formula Version Field
```json
{
  "formula": "mol-code-review",
  "version": "1.2.0",
  "min_bd_version": "0.25.0",
  ...
}
```

## Version Semantics
- Use semver: MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH
- MAJOR: Breaking changes to step structure
- MINOR: New optional steps or variables
- PATCH: Documentation, bug fixes

## Git Tag Integration
- `bd mol install repo@v1.2.0` fetches that tag
- `bd mol install repo` fetches latest tag (or main if no tags)
- `bd mol publish --tag v1.2.0` creates tag

## Proto Version Tracking
Local proto should store:
```
source_repo: github.com/anthropics/mol-code-review
source_version: v1.2.0
installed_at: 2025-12-25T12:00:00Z
```

This enables `bd mol update` to check for newer versions.

Added version comparison functions and compatibility checking during cook/pour.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dez.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1dez","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-25T12:06:42.67694-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dez.8","title":"Installation tracking: .beads/installed.json","description":"Track installed formulas for update checking and provenance.

## File Format
```json
{
  "mol-code-review": {
    "source": "github.com/anthropics/mol-code-review",
    "version": "v1.2.0",
    "installed_at": "2025-12-25T12:00:00Z",
    "formula_hash": "abc123..."
  },
  "mol-polecat-work": {
    "source": "local",
    "version": "1",
    "installed_at": "2025-12-24T10:00:00Z"
  }
}
```

## Commands That Update This
- `bd mol install` - Adds entry
- `bd mol update` - Updates version/timestamp
- `bd cook` - Adds entry with source:local
- `bd mol uninstall` - Removes entry (new command)

## Usage
```bash
bd mol list --installed    # Shows installed with source/version
bd mol outdated            # Shows formulas with available updates
```

## Sync Behavior
- installed.json is gitignored (local state)
- Only formulas/ directory is synced
- Each clone tracks its own installations

 {"id":"bd-9gvf","title":"Add prefix-based routing with routes.jsonl","description":"## Summary

When running `bd show gt-xyz` from a location where that bead doesn't exist locally,
bd should check a `routes.jsonl` file to find where beads with that prefix live.

## Problem

From ~/gt (town root):
- `bd show hq-xxx` works (hq-* beads are local)
- `bd show gt-xxx` fails (gt-* beads live in gastown/mayor/rig)

This breaks `gt hook`, `gt handoff`, and `gt prime` which need to verify beads exist.

## Solution

Add `.beads/routes.jsonl` for prefix-based routing:

\`\`\`json
{"prefix": "gt-", "path": "gastown/mayor/rig"}
{"prefix": "hq-", "path": "."}
{"prefix": "bd-", "path": "beads/crew/dave"}
\`\`\`

### Lookup Algorithm

1. Try local .beads/ first (current behavior)
2. If not found, check for routes.jsonl in current .beads/
3. Match ID prefix against routes
4. If match found, resolve path relative to routes file location and retry
5. If still not found, error as usual

### Affected Commands

Read operations that take a bead ID:
- bd show <id>
- bd update <id>
- bd close <id>
- bd dep add <id> <dep> (both IDs)

Write operations (bd create) still go to local .beads/ - no change needed.

## Implementation Notes

1. New file: routes.jsonl in .beads/ directory
2. New function: resolveBeadsPath(id string) (string, error)
3. Update: beads.Show(), beads.Update(), beads.Close(), etc. to use resolver
4. Caching: Can cache routes in memory since file rarely changes

## Context

This unblocks the gt handoff flow for Mayor. Currently gt handoff gt-xyz fails
because it can't verify the bead exists when running from town root.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-26T14:18:19.399691-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-26T14:36:43.551435-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-26T14:36:43.551435-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented prefix-based routing with routes.jsonl. Tested with gt- and bd- prefixes from town root."}

 {"id":"bd-a0cp","title":"Consider using types.Status in merge package for type safety","description":"The merge package uses string for status comparison (e.g., result.Status == closed, issue.Status == StatusTombstone). The types package defines Status as a type alias with validation. While the merge package needs its own Issue struct for JSONL flexibility, it could import and use types.Status for constants to get compile-time type checking. Current code: if left == closed || right == closed. Could be: if left == string(types.StatusClosed). This is low priority since string comparison works correctly. Files: internal/merge/merge.go:44, 488, 501-521","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-05T16:37:10.690424-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-05T16:37:10.690424-08:00"}

- bd-rgyd: Split queries.go (1586 lines)

### P3 (Low Priority):

- bd-4nqq: Remove dead test code in info_test.go
- bd-dhza: Reduce global state in main.go

## Key Areas:
1. Code duplication in path utilities
2. Large monolithic files (5 files >1000 lines)
3. Global state (25+ variables, 3 deprecated)
4. Silent error suppression (30+ instances)
5. Test gaps and dead test code

 {"id":"bd-thgk","title":"Improve test coverage for internal/compact (18.2% → 70%)","description":"Improve test coverage for internal/compact package from 17% to 70%.

## Current State
- Coverage: 17.3%
- Files: compactor.go, git.go, haiku.go
- Tests: compactor_test.go (minimal tests)

## Functions Needing Tests

### compactor.go (core compaction)
- [ ] New - needs config validation tests
- [ ] CompactTier1 - needs single issue compaction tests
- [ ] CompactTier1Batch - needs batch processing tests
- [ ] compactSingleWithResult - internal, test via public API

### git.go
- [ ] GetCurrentCommitHash - needs git repo fixture tests

### haiku.go (AI summarization) - MOCK REQUIRED
- [ ] NewHaikuClient - needs API key validation tests
- [ ] SummarizeTier1 - needs mock API response tests
- [ ] callWithRetry - needs retry logic tests
- [ ] isRetryable - needs error classification tests
- [ ] renderTier1Prompt - needs template rendering tests

## Implementation Guide

1. **Mock the Anthropic API:**
   ```go
   // Create mock HTTP server
   server := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
       json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]interface{}{
           "content": []map[string]string{{"text": "Summarized content"}},
       })
   }))
   defer server.Close()
   
   // Point client at mock
   client.baseURL = server.URL
   ```

2. **Test scenarios:**
   - Successful compaction with AI summary
   - API failure with retry
   - Rate limit handling
   - Empty issue handling
   - Large issue truncation

3. **Use test database:**
   ```go
   store, cleanup := testutil.NewTestStore(t)
   defer cleanup()
   ```

## Success Criteria
- Coverage ≥ 70%
- AI calls properly mocked (no real API calls in tests)
- Retry logic verified
- Error paths covered

## Run Tests
```bash
go test -v -cover ./internal/compact
go test -race ./internal/compact
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/Compactor","created_at":"2025-12-13T20:42:58.455767-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:41:10.80832-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T13:41:10.80832-08:00","close_reason":"Coverage improved from 17.3% to 81.8%, exceeding 70% target","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-thgk","depends_on_id":"bd-iz5t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T12:44:07.287377-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
